title: Grouped strands with consistent spacing and clean order
summary: Fiber strands route together as groups until their destinations diverge, then peel off. Shows consistent spacing between groups, clean nesting, and overall order through the routing zone.
visually_observable_good_behavior: Related fiber strands stay grouped until their destinations diverge.
Adjacent groups maintain readable spacing without lane collapse.
Strand groups remain nested and organized through horizontal runs.
Overall route order is easy to trace across the visible routing zone.

followed_requirements: Related fiber strands stay visually grouped as they move through the splice detail diagram.
Nested strands use adjacent routing lanes and move through the routing zone as an organized group.
likely_followed_rules.why_this_image_passes: Strands travel together as groups and peel off only when destinations diverge.

followed_requirements: Individual fiber strand routes maintain the 24px grid pitch.
Groups and individual strands remain visually separated and traceable.
likely_followed_rules.why_this_image_passes: Spacing between groups stays consistent and readable.

followed_requirements: Fiber strands display in standard fiber optic color-code order inside each buffer tube and nested route group.
likely_followed_rules.why_this_image_passes: Visible strand order stays organized within each group.

legacy_rule_mapping.note: Migrated from docs/reference/routing-examples/; legacy R-labels superseded by SDC IDs above.

notes: Use as a positive reference for group travel, peel-off, spacing, and order.
